original: Satoshi Tajiri, Japanesevideo game designer(Game Freak, creator of the Pokémon franchise), born in Setagaya, Japan
Japanese tech company SkyDrive says it completed the first manned test flight of a flying car
Donald Vesco rides a 21-foot-long Kawasaki motorcycle at 318.598 mph (512.73 km/h)
A geomagnetic storm causes the Aurora Borealis to shine so brightly that it is seen clearly over parts of the USA, Europe, and even as far afield as Japan

Discovery of a 3.8-million-year-old skull of the early human ancestor Australopithecus anamensis, found by Yohannes Haile-Selassie at Miro Dora, Ethiopia, upends previous evolutionary theory published in the journal "Nature"
First use of a Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) machine to scan the human body at Aberdeen Royal Infirmary, Scotland
First known photograph of a tornado is taken near Howard, South Dakota
Martin Luther King Jr.delivers his "I Have a Dream" speech during the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom civil rights march at the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C.
Climate change activistGreta Thunbergarrives in New York after sailing across the Atlantic in an emissions-free voyage
The US completes its largest dam removal project on the Klamath River at the California-Oregon border, advocated for by tribal nations to restore the salmon population
Soviet gymnastOlga Korbutbecomes a media darling at the Munich Olympics, winning gold in the team all-around, followed by two individual golds and a silver
Jack Black, American actor (High Fidelity; Kung Fu Panda), comedian, and singer (Tenacious D), born in Santa Monica, California
Kenya enacts the world's toughest ban on plastic bags with a possible US$38,000 fine and four years in jail
William Herscheldiscovers Saturn's moon Enceladus
Godfrey Hounsfield, English electrical engineer and inventor (CT scan, 1979 Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine), born in Newark, England (d. 2004)[1]
Louis Le Prince, French artist and original inventor of the motion picture camera sometimes called "the father of cinematography", born in Metz, France (d. 1890)
Brewers beat Blue Jays 22-2 with an AL record of 31 hits in 9 innings
Long Beach, California, defeats Panama for the Little League World Championship

MLB Los Angeles Angels pitcher and designated hitterShohei Ohtanibecomes the first player in team history to reach 20 stolen bases and hit 40 home runs in a season during a 10-2 win over the San Diego Padres
MLB Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber hits 4 home runs, with personal and franchise record 9 RBI's in 19-4 win over visiting Atlanta Braves at Citizens Bank Park
American 4 x 100 m freestyle relay team of David Edgar, John Murphy, John Heidenreich, andMark Spitzswim a world record 3:26.42 to beat the Soviet Union for the gold medal at the Munich Olympics
A huge upset at the Athens Olympic Stadium in the men's 4×100 m relay; Great Britain (38.07) edges the United States (38.08) to win the gold medal
Tasha Tudor, American illustrator and children's books author (Pumpkin Moonshine, Corgiville Fair), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 2008)
Leroy Chiao, Taiwanese-American engineer and astronaut (STS 65, 72, sk: 92), born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin

Journalist Clare Hollingworth observes "large numbers of troops, literally hundreds of tanks, armored cars, and field guns" Germany has aligned along the Polish border. Three days later, Hitler invades Poland and WWII begins.
The first American-built locomotive, "Tom Thumb," races a horse-drawn car from the Stockton and Stokes stagecoach company from Baltimore to Ellicott Mills; due to mechanical problems, the horse wins!
Caleb Bradham renames his carbonated soft drink "Pepsi-Cola"
First Subway sandwich shop opens in Bridgeport, Connecticut
Matt Vogel debuts as the voice of Kermit the Frog in the "Muppet Thought of the Week" video
Bobby "Boris" Pickett's song "Monster Mash" goes gold
Denmark declares a general strike against Nazi occupiers
Pharmacists John Lea and William Perrins manufacture Worcestershire sauce
Ethelda Bleibtreyleads an American medal sweep in the Antwerp Olympics women's 300 m freestyle with a world record swim of 4:34.0
The US women's basketball team goes through the Athens Olympics undefeated to win its fifth Olympic gold medal, beating Australia 74-63 in the final
Immersive AI-enhanced version of 1939 film "The Wizard of Oz" opens at Sphere in Las Vegas, Nevada
New York Cosmos defeat Seattle Sounders 2-1 at Civic Stadium in Portland, Oregon, winning their second North American Soccer League championship
Led by San Antonio Spurs shooting guard Manu Ginóbili, Argentina beats Italy 84-69 for the Olympic basketball gold medal in Athens; star-studded US team wins bronze
Carlos Tevez scores the winner as Argentina beats Paraguay 1-0, maintaining an undefeated record to win the men's football gold medal at the Athens Olympics
(Bhekizizwe) Joseph Shabalala, South African singer, songwriter, founder and musical director of Ladysmith Black Mambazo, born in Ladysmith, South Africa (d. 2020)[1][2]
Donald O'Connor, American dancer and actor (Singin' in the Rain), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2003)
Jennifer Coolidge, American actress and comedian (Legally Blonde film series; Christopher Guest films), born in Boston, Massachusetts
"I'll Be There" single by The Jackson 5 is released (Billboard Song of the Year 1970)
Tony Sheridanand The Beat Brothers (also known as The Beatles) record "Ya Ya" (Parts 1 & 2) in Hamburg, Germany

Teresa Milanollo, Italian violin prodigy, and  composer, born in Savigliano, Piedmont, Kingdom of Sardinia (d. 1904)
Vonda N. McIntyre, American sci-fi author (Star Trek II, III, IV), born in Louisville, Kentucky
MLB California Angels pitcherNolan Ryanstrikes out 300 batters for the fifth consecutive year

American swimmer Norman Ross wins his second of three gold medals at the Antwerp Olympics, beating teammate Ludwig Langer in the men's 400 m freestyle, and also wins the 1500 m and 4 x 200 m freestyle relay
Orestes López, Cuban band leader and composer (co-inventor of 'mambo' with his brother, Cachao), born in Havana, Cuba (d. 1991)
Test cricket debut of Muttiah Muralitharan against Australia in Colombo
Last day of Test Cricket for Richie Richardson
Rita Dove, African-American poet (Thomas and Beulah), born in Akron, Ohio

George Church, American geneticist and synthetic biologist, born in MacDill Air Force Base, Florida

9th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Chicago Bears 21, All-Stars 0 (101,100 attendees)
